Code fragment: case LIKE -> field.like(fc.getValue().toString());
Explanation: Using LIKE with a user-provided value may perform full-text search if the value contains wildcards (% or _). Consider using a dedicated full-text search engine like Elasticsearch or PostgreSQL's full-text search capabilities instead.

Code fragment: case STRING_LOOSE_EQUAL -> field.like("%" + fc.getValue() + "%");
Explanation: Using LIKE with wildcards on both sides ('%value%') performs inefficient full-text search that cannot use indexes. Consider using a dedicated full-text search solution like PostgreSQL's tsvector/tsquery or Elasticsearch.

Code fragment: dslContext.select(Arrays.asList(table.fields())).from(table),
Explanation: Selecting all columns from a table using table.fields() fetches every column regardless of what's actually needed. Explicitly specify only the required columns to reduce data transfer and improve query performance.

Code fragment:
Result<CoreFileSystemRecord> result = this.context
                .selectFrom(CORE_FILE_SYSTEM)
Explanation: Using selectFrom() fetches all columns from CORE_FILE_SYSTEM table, but only a subset of fields are actually needed (ID, NAME, PARENT_ID based on usage). Consider using select() with only the required columns to improve query performance.

Code fragment:
var records = this.context
                .selectFrom(CORE_FILE_SYSTEM)
Explanation: Using selectFrom() retrieves all columns from CORE_FILE_SYSTEM, but only specific fields (ID, NAME, FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE, SIZE, CREATED_AT, UPDATED_AT) are used for FileDetail. Use select() with explicit column list to reduce data transfer.

Code fragment: if (!StringUtil.safeIsBlank(filter)) conditions.add(CORE_FILE_SYSTEM.NAME.like("%" + filter + "%"));
Explanation: Using LIKE with wildcards on both sides ('%filter%') for text filtering performs a full table scan and cannot use indexes efficiently. Consider using a full-text search index or database-specific text search capabilities for better performance.

Code fragment: .selectFrom(CORE_USERS)
Explanation: Using selectFrom() fetches all columns from the CORE_USERS table, which may retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Consider using select() with only the specific columns needed for the User object.

Code fragment: public final TableField<CoreAuthoritiesRecord, Long> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("core.core_next_id()"), SQLDataType.BIGINT)), this, "");

Consider using 'name' as the primary key or renaming 'id' to something more descriptive like 'authority_id'.

Code fragment: public final TableField<CoreFileSystemRecord, FileResourceType> FILE_RESOURCE_TYPE = createField(DSL.name("file_resource_type"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR.nullable(false).defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("'STATIC'::core_file_resource_type"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR)).asEnumDataType(CoreFileResourceType.class), this, "", new EnumConverter<CoreFileResourceType, FileResourceType>(CoreFileResourceType.class, FileResourceType.class));
